risks.title: The work becomes too centrally resource-intensive
risks.description: Central teams cannot directly support a review for every course or manually sustain all pack production.
risks.mitigation: Use a common self-run workshop and clear guidance, with central effort focused on evidence and method, focal-area support delivered through faculty-linked Careers Consultants, and broader-rollout support available through normal business-partner relationships.

risks.title: Data is over-interpreted or used punitively
risks.description: Small numbers, suppression, volatility, coding issues or comparator misuse could lead to unfair or unhelpful interpretations.
risks.mitigation: Use clear caveats, responsible-use principles, workshop guidance and plain language throughout the pack and review.

risks.title: Course claim becomes unsupported marketing language
risks.description: Student-facing or applicant-facing language could drift beyond the evidence or lose academic ownership.
risks.mitigation: Require evidence-linked proof points and course confirmation before claim material is used more visibly.

risks.title: Faculty engagement is inconsistent
risks.description: Local facilitation, supported review and self-review may vary in quality, tone and completion across faculties.
risks.mitigation: Use a common pack and self-run workshop, faculty-linked Careers Consultant support, responsible-use guidance, transparent coverage reporting and an agreed exception route.

risks.title: Prioritisation is perceived as punitive
risks.description: Courses selected for the first rollout may feel labelled as weak or problematic.
risks.mitigation: Use a transparent selection process that considers evidence, faculty judgement, course context, strengths, learning value and support needs.

risks.title: The evidence resource is not sustained
risks.description: The pack, visualisation, refresh rhythm and guidance could remain dependent on project goodwill after initial delivery.
risks.mitigation: Confirm the refresh timetable, owner, access route, interpretation guidance and quality enhancement monitoring handoff before the second rollout is complete.

risks.title: Self-run review lacks enough support
risks.description: Courses may receive the pack and workshop materials without enough guidance or support to use them well.
risks.mitigation: Provide clear guidance, responsible-use materials and a route for help where evidence is unclear or sensitive.
risks.owner: Rachel Kershaw and the King's Academy Academic Development Lead

issues.title: Project management capacity is not yet secured
issues.description: Step 1 will proceed through minimal interim coordination by Mark Peace, Rachel Kershaw and Daniel Robson. Borrowed Transformation Office project management capacity is required from October 2026 at 0.2 FTE, reducing to 0.1 FTE from July 2027.

issues.title: Named specialist contributors need confirmation
issues.description: Rachel Kershaw needs to confirm the Data Manager contribution and nominate the Careers Workshop, Pilot and Mobilisation Lead. Daniel Robson needs to secure a named King's Academy Academic Development Lead and Academic Quality needs to nominate an operational CER contact.
issues.neededBy: Before substantive design and pilot activity

issues.title: Actual focal areas remain to be selected
issues.description: The selection process and decision route are agreed, but the feasible Careers capacity envelope, evidence-informed proposal and Education Executive decision are still required.

issues.title: Operational data access and handover need confirmation
issues.description: The intended Careers BAU ownership is agreed, but the named enrolment-data owner, Fabric permissions, data access, CER workflow and documented technical and assurance handover still need to be completed.
issues.owner: Rachel Kershaw with Mark Peace during Step 1
issues.neededBy: Access before the internship; handover before BAU transfer

assumptions.title: Evidence can support both claims and enhancement
assumptions.summary: The same evidence can help courses articulate a stronger graduate futures claim and identify where student support could be strengthened.

assumptions.title: Existing Careers and local arrangements can support scale
assumptions.summary: The project assumes that the existing Careers Consultant business-partner model can provide higher-touch focal-area support and remain available on request during broader rollout, while programmes arrange their own local participation.

assumptions.title: Early course partners will improve the approach
assumptions.summary: Early course partners will help test whether the pack and self-run workshop are useful, fair, proportionate and academically credible.

assumptions.title: Shared prioritisation can be agreed constructively
assumptions.summary: Focal courses for the first rollout can be agreed through evidence, faculty knowledge, course context and local judgement.

assumptions.title: Programmes can run the review using the provided materials
assumptions.summary: Programmes can use the pack, self-run workshop and guidance with proportionate support from their linked Careers Consultant where needed.

assumptions.title: Course confirmation supports wider use of a claim
assumptions.summary: Course teams can confirm that student-facing or applicant-facing claim language is accurate, fair and evidence-informed before local use.

assumptions.title: The evidence resource can become a maintained service
assumptions.summary: The deliverable assumes that visualisation maintenance, data refresh, interpretation guidance and access routes can move from project delivery into a sustainable institutional route.
